contentBlocks.original:
कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय हरये परमात्मने
प्रणतः क्लेशनाशाय गोविंदाय नमोनमः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛṣṇāya vāsudevāya haraye paramātmane
praṇataḥ kleśanāśāya govindāya namo namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Krishna, the son of Vasudeva, the supreme soul, who removes all suffering for those who surrender to him. I bow repeatedly to Govinda, the one who alleviates all hardships.
contentBlocks.meanings: कृष्णाय (kṛṣṇāya) – to Krishna; वासुदेवाय (vāsudevāya) – to Vasudeva's son (vāsudeva:Vasudeva's | āya:to); हरये (haraye) – to Hari; परमात्मने (paramātmane) – to the supreme soul (parama:supreme | ātmane:to the soul); प्रणतः (praṇataḥ) – bowed down; क्लेशनाशाय (kleśanāśāya) – to the remover of suffering (kleśa:suffering | nāśāya:to the remover); गोविंदाय (govindāya) – to Govinda; नमोनमः (namo namaḥ) – salutations repeatedly (namaḥ:salutations | namo:repeatedly);
